purpose: One-shot, pre-refactor capture of the ALLOWLIST subtrees (colors, semanticTokens, fonts, fontSizes, radii, styles, config, components.<Name>.defaultProps for Button/Tabs/Progress/Badge/Tag/Checkbox/Radio) of getChakraTheme(id) for all five current themes (calm, playful, lego, minecraft, flower), taken from the unmodified app/src/lib/theme.ts. The committed output is the frozen AC-PARITY-1 parity fixture that story S2 asserts against after the theme.ts refactor lands. This module is run exactly once, now, before S1 touches theme.ts; it is not wired into the ongoing test suite or prebuild pipeline.

public_api.name: capture-theme-baseline.mjs (CLI entry point)
public_api.description: Imports the current app/src/lib/theme.ts's getChakraTheme export (via an in-memory TypeScript-to-JS transpile using the already-installed `typescript` devDependency's transpileModule API, written to a throwaway temp .mjs under app/scripts/ so Node's ESM loader can execute it, then deleted), computes the ALLOWLIST pick for each of the five theme ids, asserts the picked object is function-free and JSON-round-trip-lossless (throws a non-zero-exit error otherwise, or if getChakraTheme is undefined/throws for any id), and writes app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts. Deterministic: re-running against an unmodified theme.ts reproduces byte-identical output (verified by diffing two consecutive runs).

public_api.name: baseline
public_api.description: The committed parity fixture in app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts. Keyed by theme id; each value is exactly the ALLOWLIST subtree pick (colors, semanticTokens, fonts, fontSizes, radii, styles, config, components.{Button,Tabs,Progress,Badge,Tag,Checkbox,Radio}.defaultProps). Consumed by story S2's AC-PARITY-1 test via `baseline[id]` (matches the pseudocode in spec.md §6.8 / architecture.md Implementation Constraint #4).

public_api.name: ThemeBaselineAllowlist
public_api.signature: export type ThemeBaselineAllowlist = { colors: unknown; semanticTokens: unknown; fonts: unknown; fontSizes: unknown; radii: unknown; styles: unknown; config: unknown; components: { Button: { defaultProps: unknown }; Tabs: { defaultProps: unknown }; Progress: { defaultProps: unknown }; Badge: { defaultProps: unknown }; Tag: { defaultProps: unknown }; Checkbox: { defaultProps: unknown }; Radio: { defaultProps: unknown } } }
public_api.description: Type of each per-theme entry in `baseline`. Fields are typed `unknown` rather than mirroring theme.ts's private input types, since this module must not import or depend on theme.ts's internal (non-exported) shapes -- only on the public getChakraTheme(id) return value, which itself is Chakra's ThemeOverride (not narrowed further here).

owned_data: app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts (the committed parity baseline: exclusively owned/regenerated by this script; no other module writes to this path)
dependencies_allowed: app/src/lib/theme.ts (read-only, via a one-time transpile+dynamic-import at capture time -- reads the *current*, unmodified getChakraTheme/APP_THEMES export; never edits it)

typescript (already an app/package.json devDependency; used only for its transpileModule API to strip TS syntax so a plain Node ESM script can execute theme.ts's source -- not tsx/ts-node/@swc, no new dependency added)
node:fs, node:fs/promises, node:path, node:url, node:crypto (temp-file handling, output write)
dependencies_forbidden: app/src/themes/* (does not exist yet -- would violate 'no edits to theme.ts, capture works against the current export as-is' and would pre-empt S1/S2)
tsx, ts-node, @swc/* or any new build dependency beyond the already-installed `typescript` and `zod` (zod not used here) -- spec D5

vitest / the test runner (this is a standalone script the architect/dev runs once, not a test; wiring it into the suite would let a later run silently re-capture post-refactor output and erase the parity guarantee)
test_targets: app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts is not itself tested in S0 (writing the AC-PARITY-1 assertion is explicitly out of scope, landing in S2). S0's verification is: (a) the script runs to completion with exit code 0, (b) re-running it produces a byte-identical file (idempotence), (c) `make test-unit` baseline count is unchanged (no new tests added, no regressions), (d) app/src/lib/theme.ts has zero diff from its pre-S0 state.
notes: Boundary-check finding (non-blocking): architecture.md's Module Map row for 'baseline capture' and Implementation Constraint #4 both explicitly authorize this module to import the current getChakraTheme from app/src/lib/theme.ts, but the terse 'Allowed dependency edges' bullet list under Boundary Rules does not separately restate this edge (that list enumerates the app/src/themes/* infra's ongoing edges). Since the same authoritative document unambiguously grants this edge in two other places, this is treated as a documentation completeness gap in the terse list, not an unresolved seam -- proceeding without escalation. No live Architect teammate was reachable in this spawn to message about it; flagged here and in the story's post-impl verification notes instead.

Mechanism choice for 'plain .mjs cannot import .ts' (documented per the story's explicit request to justify this choice): evaluated three options -- (1) shelling out to vitest as a one-shot runner: rejected because it would either require a committed test file that re-runs the capture on every `make test-unit` invocation (silently overwriting the frozen baseline once S1 edits theme.ts) or an uncommitted throwaway test file invoked ad hoc, which is harder to reproduce/audit than a plain script; (2) esbuild: rejected because it is a *transitive*, native (platform-specific) dependency not declared in app/package.json -- this project's CLAUDE.md explicitly flags native-binary cross-platform risk (rolldown, @next/swc) as a live concern, and depending on an undeclared transitive native binary would reintroduce exactly that risk; (3) TypeScript compiler's `transpileModule` API (chosen): `typescript` is already a direct devDependency, is pure JS (no native binary, so no cross-platform stamp risk), and theme.ts's only non-erasable import is the real npm package `@chakra-ui/react` (its one cross-module import, `@/src/types`, is `import type`-only and is fully erased by the transpile, so no path-alias resolution is needed at runtime). Verified empirically: all five themes transpile, dynamic-import, and JSON-round-trip cleanly with zero functions inside the ALLOWLIST subtrees, confirming spec §6.8's empirical claim.
